title: ruthiella subcordata
intro: Ruthiella subcordata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the "Ginger Lily" or "Water Ginger." The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Ruthiella subcordata include relieving digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and aiding in the management of various infections, making it a valuable herbal remedy for overall well-being. Traditional herbal preparations of Ruthiella subcordata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, added to food, or used topically for skin conditions and wound healing.
